event_blocks.summary: The men's high jump final at the World Athletics Championships Oregon 22 in Eugene, Oregon, USA, commenced under bright sunlight in a packed stadium with a palpable, vibrant, and competitive atmosphere. Announcers welcomed a global audience to the event, detailing the starting lineup of 13 athletes, including top contenders Sanghyeok WOO (KOR, world rank 2) and Gianmarco TAMBERI (ITA, world rank 1). Mutaz Essa BARSHIM (QAT), known as 'the prince' and a two-time defending champion, appeared focused, standing near the track in a red jacket. TAMBERI, the Italian athlete in a blue shirt, also appeared relaxed, interacting casually with BARSHIM. Edgar RIVERA (MEX), identified as world rank 20, age 31, with a personal best of 2.31m, was the first to jump. Other athletes introduced included Joel BADEN (AUS), Luis Enrique ZAYAS (CUB), Yonathan KAPITOLNIK (ISR), Mateusz PRZYBYLKO (GER), Tomohiro SHINNO (JPN), Django LOVETT (CAN), Andriy PROTSENKO (UKR), and American athletes Shelby MCEWEN and JuVaughn HARRISON. The stadium featured a red track, a colorful landing mat, 'OREGON 22' banners, and a large, engaged audience. As the competition progressed, the on-screen scoreboard showed results, with RIVERA marked as having failed an attempt at 2.19m, a height contrasted with previous qualifying marks. The scene concluded with a tense yet celebratory atmosphere reflecting the culmination of the final.

event_blocks.summary: In the sunlit outdoor stadium of the World Athletics Championships Oregon 22, with 'OREGON 22' banners and the bar set at 2.24m, the Men's High Jump Final is underway amidst tense crowd noise and commentary. Edgar RIVERA, the Mexican athlete in a red 'TDK' top, executes a powerful approach, clearing the bar with a technique described as 'Rivera with a brush,' 'brush but good fortune,' and 'good stretch of the legs,' noting his 'hips were up, coming down' and he 'got it with his bottom' despite his 'height just a little bit out in front.' After a successful jump, Rivera raises his fist, but later shows visible frustration, wiping his face after a failed attempt at 2.19m. Yonathan KAPITOLNIK, the Israeli athlete in a blue and white striped top, identified as 'The young man from Israel, Kapitolnik again,' also clears the 2.24m bar cleanly with a focused stride, landing smoothly and walking away calmly, adjusting his hair, as officials and a Photographer with a Tripod observe. Commentators provide background on Rivera's collegiate career at the University of Arizona, noting his familiarity with the facility. Despite raising his fist in celebration after an earlier success, Rivera appears frustrated after a failed 2.19m jump, and on his third attempt, he fails to clear the bar, knocking it down, eliciting crowd roars. Spectators, including a Spectator in Green Shirt and a Spectator in Red Shirt, react with concern and anticipation. The scoreboard shows final standings with multiple athletes tied at 2.24m, while Rivera's failed 2.19m attempt is marked 'XX'.

raw_segments: The video captures a dramatic moment at the Men's High Jump Final, opening with a wide shot of the packed World Athletics Championships Oregon 22 stadium under bright sunlight, filled with the sounds of a cheering and clapping crowd. Gianmarco TAMBERI, the Italian athlete in a blue 'ITALIA' top, executes a powerful run-up and successfully clears the bar with a Fosbury Flop. As he lands on the mat, rolls over, and lies flat on his back in celebration, arms outstretched, commentators identify him by name, praising his "great performance" and noting his three third-attempt clearances. They recall his gold medal from the 2016 World Indoor Championships, but clarify that in this event, he "did not medal" but "did not disappoint either," achieving his "highest finish at a World Championship." Tamberi sits up, raises his arms triumphantly, and walks along the red track, waving to the enthusiastic crowd, which includes fans waving an American flag and an 'ITALIA GOOD' sign. The atmosphere is electric, with stadium branding like 'OREGON 22' prominently displayed, as Tamberi continues to bask in the moment, acknowledging the appreciation for his fine competition.

The Men's High Jump Final at the World Athletics Championships Oregon 22 unfolds under bright sunlight in a packed stadium, with 'OREGON 22' banners visible. The segment opens with Gianmarco TAMBERI, the Italian athlete in a blue sleeveless top, executing a powerful Fosbury Flop over the 2.35m bar. An announcer notes his distinctive ponytail as he begins, stating "nothing else after that mattered" as he clears it cleanly. TAMBERI lands triumphantly, lying flat before rising to raise his arms and wave to the cheering crowd, while the scoreboard confirms his successful '2.35' jump. The announcer updates the standings, mentioning Barshim in gold and Woo in silver with a 2.35m clearance. Officials then adjust the bar to 2.37m. The focus shifts to Andriy PROTSENKO, the Ukrainian athlete in a bright yellow top, whom the announcer identifies as "Protsenko from the Ukraine," noting his previous clearance at 2.33m and two misses at 2.35m, leaving him with "one shot at 2.37m." PROTSENKO runs with intense focus, then clears the 2.37m bar with a clean Fosbury Flop, prompting the announcer to exclaim, "One shot at it and what a shot it was!" as he describes PROTSENKO getting "his hips up over it." PROTSENKO lands confidently, raising his arms in celebration to the erupting applause from the packed stadium. He walks away with a composed, satisfied expression. The camera captures the enthusiastic crowd, including a South Korean fan group with a large banner for WOO and a spectator in a 'USA' shirt clapping, highlighting the intense competition and emotional highs of the final jumps.

The video opens with Andriy PROTSENKO, the Ukrainian athlete in a bright yellow sleeveless top with 'UKRAINE' and 'PROTSENKO' on his bib, walking alongside an Italian athlete in a blue shirt. A commentator notes that "the bronze medal will be his," adding, "He knows what that bronze medal means to Protsenko, to his country." The scene then shifts to Mutaz Essa BARSHIM, the Qatari athlete in a red and white striped singlet and gold-tinted sunglasses, standing focused on the track. The scoreboard overlay shows the men's high jump final standings, with BARSHIM leading at 2.35m and PROTSENKO at 2.33m. The commentator builds anticipation, mentioning a "man who's jumped 243, second highest jumper in the history of the sport." BARSHIM adjusts his sunglasses, takes a deep breath, and prepares for his attempt at 2.37m, which the commentator confirms as his "first attempt 237." With intense focus, he begins his run-up, executing a clean Fosbury Flop over the bar, clearing it successfully. The crowd erupts in cheers as he lands on the mat, raises his arms in celebration, and points upward, prompting a "Wow!" from the commentator. The vibrant stadium environment, with packed stands, bright sunlight, and 'OREGON 22' banners, is filled with the electric atmosphere of the final jump, while a SEIKO scoreboard in the background displays 'TRIPLE JUMP W FINAL'.

The segment opens with Mutaz Essa BARSHIM, the Qatari high jumper, preparing for his attempt at the World Athletics Championships Oregon 22. Dressed in his red and white singlet and gold-tinted sunglasses, he adjusts his eyewear, takes deep breaths, and raises his hands in focused anticipation, as a commentator praises him as "one of the all-time greats" amidst the murmuring crowd. The camera briefly cuts to the enthusiastic spectators, including a fan with an American flag and a Qatari official in a red jacket and pink cap celebrating, while the commentator expresses hope that "everybody that watches that in this country, everybody in the stadium appreciates what they're seeing and appreciates this athlete." Returning to BARSHIM, the commentator explicitly names him as he executes a powerful run-up and performs a flawless Fosbury Flop, clearing the 2.37m bar with "room to spare again." The commentator highlights his remarkable consistency, noting that "each jump" has looked "just the same" across six attempts, with "clearances over the bar." BARSHIM lands triumphantly on the mat, raising his arms and pointing upward in celebration, his victory captured from multiple angles against the bright, sunny stadium and clear blue sky. The scoreboard updates to show his successful 2.37m (O) jump, placing him in first. The palpable energy of the crowd, with fans clapping and waving flags, including a South Korean fan group with a banner for "WOO 우상혁," underscores the grandeur of the moment, as the commentator briefly mentions "Woosanghyeok" before the segment concludes with BARSHIM still celebrating.

event_blocks.summary: In the Men's High Jump Final at the World Athletics Championships Oregon 22, Sanghyeok WOO of South Korea, wearing a white 'KOREA' tank top and orange shorts, initially appeared to secure gold with a successful 2.39m Fosbury Flop, celebrated with raised arms and shouts as the crowd erupted, with the SEIKO scoreboard reflecting his lead. However, commentators clarified that Mutaz Essa BARSHIM of Qatar, who had previously cleared 2.37m, was the gold medalist, with WOO earning silver and Andriy PROTSENKO taking bronze, while Gianmarco TAMBERI and Shelby MCEWEN were also acknowledged for their performances. Barshim, after his victory, embraced TAMBERI and was congratulated by PROTSENKO and Joel BADEN, before confidently preparing for his attempts, clearing 2.37m with a triumphant gesture and crowd applause, though a subsequent attempt was declared 'No.' Barshim later walked composedly, retrieving his belongings, donning a red QATAR shirt and cap, and exclaiming "I am high jump" before unfurling the Qatari flag in a proud display, cementing his status as a three-time consecutive World Champion. The stadium environment, under bright sunlight with 'OREGON 22' banners, buzzed with celebratory energy throughout the competition, with the final results graphic confirming BARSHIM's first place at 2.37m, WOO second at 2.35m, and PROTSENKO and TAMBERI tied at 2.33m, with other athletes like Edgar RIVERA and Mateusz PRZYBYLKO also noted.

raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of Sanghyeok WOO, the South Korean high jumper, displaying intense focus and emotion as he prepares for his final 2.39m attempt. The scoreboard shows him ranked second behind Mutaz Essa BARSHIM. WOO, in his white 'KOREA' tank top and orange shorts, is seen concentrating while commentators discuss his rivalry with Barshim, recalling past Diamond League meets in Doha where WOO beat him on his 'home turf,' and Barshim's desire to win gold and take it back to Qatar. The camera then cuts to a wider shot of the packed stadium under bright sunlight, with 'OREGON 22' banners visible. WOO begins his powerful run-up, executing a clean Fosbury Flop over the 2.39m bar. He lands on the mat, immediately raising his arms in triumph and shouting in celebration. The crowd erupts in cheers, with a distinct 'WOO!' shout heard, as WOO runs joyfully around the track, waving to fans, including a visible South Korean fan group. The atmosphere is electric, enhanced by lens flares. Visually, the SEIKO scoreboard updates to reflect his successful jump, marking him as the new leader in the competition. However, the commentators declare, 'It's the gold medal there, the gold medal belongs again to Mutaz Essa Barshim,' adding that 'Woo Sang-yuk' made 'a great effort for silver,' even as WOO continues to celebrate his apparent victory with a beaming face.

The video opens with a scene of camaraderie and sportsmanship as the Qatari Athlete, identified by the announcer as 'Barshim, the champ,' wearing a red jacket with 'QATAR' on the back and a white cap, embraces the Italian Athlete, named 'Tambery' by the announcer, who is in a blue shirt with an Italian flag patch, near the track. The announcer also mentions 'the bronze medalist, Protsenko' and 'Harrison,' adding to the atmosphere of elite competition. The warm and celebratory mood is underscored by the presence of other athletes and officials, including an Official in White Shirt, in the background. The scene then shifts to Sanghyeok WOO, the South Korean athlete in a white tank top with 'KOREA' and 'WOO' printed on it, who is seen wiping his face and walking with intense focus. The camera then cuts to Mutaz Essa BARSHIM, the Qatari athlete in a red and white striped singlet with 'QATAR' and 'BARSHIM' on the front, wearing gold-tinted sunglasses, standing near the high jump area. The announcer explicitly identifies him as 'Mutaz Essa Barshim' and highlights his impressive record, stating he 'hasn't missed' three in qualifying and is aiming for 'six here tonight in the final to win.' The scoreboard displays his name and a height of 2.37m, and he raises his arms in celebration, signaling a successful jump. The crowd erupts in applause, described by the announcer as 'on its feet, clapping in unison,' as BARSHIM walks confidently toward the bar, preparing for his attempt, which the announcer calls 'the one shot.' The camera captures his powerful run-up, his clean Fosbury Flop over the 2.37m bar, and his triumphant landing on the mat. He raises his arms in victory, pointing upward, as the crowd cheers. The announcer then declares 'No,' indicating a failed subsequent attempt by Barshim. The scene then shifts to WOO, who is seen executing a powerful run-up and successfully clearing the 2.39m bar with a Fosbury Flop, landing on the mat and celebrating with raised arms, shouting in joy. The South Korean Fan Group in the stands, holding a large white banner with the South Korean flag and Korean text, waves and cheers enthusiastically. The SEIKO Scoreboard in the background displays 'WOO' and '2.39', confirming his successful attempt, while the announcer frequently references strategic heights like '2.37,' '2.39,' and '2.42' throughout the segment. The video concludes with BARSHIM and WOO both celebrating their achievements, with the crowd's energy reaching a peak in the consistent Stadium Environment, a large outdoor stadium with a red track, colorful landing mat, packed spectator stands, and banners reading 'OREGON 22' and 'World Athletics Championships Oregon 22', under bright sunlight with strong shadows and a clear blue sky.

The post-competition scene at the Oregon 22 World Athletics Championships captures Mutaz Essa BARSHIM, the Qatari high jump athlete, walking away from the high jump area with a composed demeanor. As he moves along the red track, passing officials and photographers, a commentator remarks, "That'll do it," and anticipates him taking his medal and flowers, noting "bouquets from the crowd." Barshim approaches his belongings, and the commentator declares, "Barshim again, he'll stamp himself... He's one of the greatest all time." He bends down to pick up a red jacket, then puts on a red QATAR-branded shirt over his singlet, adjusting his cap and gold-tinted sunglasses. A distinct voice exclaims, "I am high jump!" which the commentator affirms, "Yes he is. I am high jump." Barshim then retrieves the Qatari national flag, unfurls it with both arms raised in celebration, and walks proudly across the track, draped in the flag. The stadium is filled with spectators under bright sunlight, with 'OREGON 22' banners visible, as the commentator highlights Barshim's historic achievement: "Champion in 2017, champion in 2019. The only man ever to win back-to-back World Championships in the high jump, he's now gone back-to-back to back," all while the crowd's celebratory buzz fills the air.

The segment opens with a wide-angle view of the packed stadium under bright sunlight and a clear blue sky during the Men's High Jump Final at the World Athletics Championships Oregon 22. An on-screen graphic displays the final results, showing Mutaz Essa BARSHIM (QAT) in first place with 2.37m, Sanghyeok WOO (KOR) at 2.35m, and Andriy PROTSENKO (UKR) and Gianmarco TAMBERI (ITA) both at 2.33m. A commentator's voice confirms these standings, stating, "The gold medalist was 31, the silver medalist 26, the bronze medalist is Protsenko." He then points out, "There they are. Barshim, Woo, Protsenko," and adds that "Tamberi helped give the fans a show with his clearances, with his personality, with his charisma." The commentator also acknowledges "Shelby McEwen in a fine championship as he went through," and mentions "a lot of guys at 227" as the scoreboard updates to include positions 11 to 13, showing Yonathan KAPITOLNIK (ISR) and Mateusz PRZYBYLKO (GER) tied at 2.24m. He specifically notes, "Edgar Rivera, Mexico, 219, made the opening height." The graphic remains static, emphasizing the final standings, as the commentator concludes, "the championship belongs to Barshim." The scene then transitions to a black screen featuring the colorful 'World Athletics Championships Oregon 22' logo and social media handles, followed by the hashtag '#WORLDATHLETICSCHAMPS' against abstract shapes, marking the event's celebratory and conclusive end.
